title: arrays in state schemas must use mandatory notation
content:
# arrays in state schemas must use mandatory notation

In GraphQL state schemas, arrays follow a strict convention:

```graphql
type ServiceOfferingState {
    tiers: [Tier!]!        # ✅ Correct — both inner and outer are required
    items: [Item]           # ❌ Wrong — must use [Item!]!
}
```

Objects inside arrays must include an `OID!` field for unique identification:

```graphql
type Tier {
    id: OID!               # Required for array items
    name: String
    label: OLabel          # Optional but useful for metadata
}
```

The `OID!` field enables operations to target specific items within arrays (e.g., "update the tier with this ID"). Without it, there is no stable way to reference individual array elements across operations.

---

Topics:
- [[schema design]]
description: Arrays must be declared as [ObjectType!]! and objects within arrays must include an OID! field for unique identification
